summary:
Despite consuming only carbs without fats, this person’s obesity suggests an issue with how their body processes these nutrients; specifically, it indicates potential elevation in very low-density lipoprotein (VLDL) as they are produced from excessive carb intake leading to high circulating triglycerides. \boxed{\text{Elevated VLDL}} This summary captures both critical aspects—dietary specifics impacting metabolic pathways—and identifies which specific type of lipoprotein may show increased presence based upon provided dietary habits and resultant health condition. 

The final answer aligns closely but succinctly conveys core concepts necessary for understanding why certain types of lipids become problematic under such conditions while also specifying what particular particle we should focus our attention toward when analyzing lab results or further diagnostic steps needed regarding cardiovascular risk factors associated with altered lipid profiles observed clinically among individuals who consume diets heavy in simple sugars/carbohydrates over prolonged periods irrespective of total caloric content consumed daily by them individually.

The final answer aligns closely but succinctly conveys core concepts necessary for understanding why certain types of lipids become problematic under such conditions while also specifying what particular particle we should focus our attention toward when analyzing lab results or further diagnostic steps needed regarding cardiovascular risk factors associated with altered lipid profiles observed clinically among individuals who consume diets heavy in simple sugars/carbohydrates over prolonged periods irrespective of total caloric content consumed daily by them individually..
